mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and financing costs, so higher policy-rate momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 5.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward coefficient on 3-month FEDFUNDS changes is nonnegative, or if high-rate-momentum months produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum crosses above a high threshold, investors price a higher probability of restrictive policy persistence, reducing equity multiples and causing negative short-horizon S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
fact:CPIAUCSL latest value 314.121 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have mean 5-day forward returns greater than the unconditional mean, or if the trigger hit-rate for negative returns is below 50% in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS indicates labor demand deterioration, which lowers expected earnings growth and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3pp while PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change crosses below +100k
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:UNRATE latest value 4.2 as of t-1mo
fact:PAYEMS latest value 158779.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 60-day returns than either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real retail momentum, measured by RSAFS rising faster than CPIAUCSL, signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS divided by CPIAUCSL 3-month growth crosses above +1.0%
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:RSAFS latest value 709668.0 as of t-2mo
fact:CPIAUCSL latest value 314.121 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if real RSAFS momentum has a nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year walk-forward window, or if triggered returns underperform baseline by more than 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO momentum reflects expanding physical output and operating leverage, so stronger 6-month industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest value 102.8887 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the coefficient on 6-month INDPRO growth is negative in walk-forward regressions, or if positive INDPRO momentum fails to outperform negative momentum over two consecutive 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A break lower in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1200 and 12-month change is negative
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest value 1238.0 as of t-2mo
regime_hint: housing-led slowdown under tight financial conditions
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside breaks are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth remains strong despite elevated FEDFUNDS, earnings resilience dominates discount-rate pressure and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized q/q growth crosses above +2.0% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:GDPC1 latest value 22924.863 as of t-5mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 5.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-growth high-rate condition has lower 60-day returns than high FEDFUNDS alone, or if its positive-return frequency is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.0% while FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%, restrictive policy coincides with labor-market slack, increasing recession-risk pricing and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.0% while FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:UNRATE latest value 4.2 as of t-1mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 5.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if this joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations, or if adding FEDFUNDS does not improve predictive accuracy versus UNRATE alone.
